name: Grover Mitchell
abstract: Grover Mitchell, born Grover Curry Mitchell (March 17, 1930 – August 6, 2003) was an American jazz trombonist who led the Count Basie Orchestra.

interviews.title: Grover Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, February 13, 1996

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie
Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie
JVC Jazz Festival: Latin Jazz Jam

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Buddy and Al Viola and Al Aarons and Grover Mitchell and Leroy Vinnegar had a label together.

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions 'the Count Basie Orchestra led by Grover Mitchell at the time' only in response to the interviewer's statement about hearing the band at a festival. The interviewee does not claim personal interaction, collaboration, or acquaintance with Grover Mitchell.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Metz Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: He took it to the gig and gave it to Grover... I had all my information on the cassette. And what had happened was Grover was listening to it on the bus and he said, “hey Basie, here, listen to this.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Metz Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 3, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Grover Mitchell, the current leader of the Basie orchestra who’s doing a wonderful job and has got the band still sounding like a top-notch group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And the trombone player, Grover... Grover Mitchell, yeah. He was in the Navy. In fact, I think we were the first Bebop band they had ever heard!

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: Grover Mitchell was never in the band when I was in the band, but Grover Mitchell he did some leading
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
